title: NCCL arXiv papers scheduled crawler refresh, 2026-07-12
scope_note: Three local arXiv abstract snapshots captured by the scheduled NCCL arXiv crawler. This manifest records abstract-level discovery evidence only; it is not a full-paper review, benchmark reproduction, production evidence, or replacement for NCCL release notes, issue records, or NVIDIA technical-blog evidence.

duplicate_boundary.prior_curated_scope: The existing NCCL Arxiv Papers page indexed the 2026-07-05 scheduled batch and represented DynamiQ through the older 2602.08923v1 capture.
reconciliation: CTA-Pipelining 2607.07862v1 has no prior title, canonical URL, arXiv identity, or content-hash match in the local NCCL arXiv captures.
Adaptive Space-efficient Collectives 2607.04676v1 has no prior title, canonical URL, arXiv identity, or content-hash match in the local NCCL arXiv captures.

DynamiQ 2602.08923v3 shares the unversioned arXiv identity and title with the older 2602.08923v1 captures, but has canonical_url http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08923v3, updated_at 2026-07-07T20:21:24Z, and content_hash 4ed352c0df50d5d26ccc31cf3f069d5b0912331882decfa02b8f1fa75b346a27, so it is recorded as a version refresh rather than a new standalone paper.

papers.title: CTA-Pipelining: A Latency-Oriented Spatial Scaling Method for Multi-GPU Systems
abstract_claims: latency-oriented spatial scaling for shared-memory multi-GPU systems serving LLMs
uses CUTLASS, cuBLAS, and NCCL on 8-GPU H200 and B200 systems
abstract reports up to 31.8% latency reduction versus micro-batching and 29.6% versus tensor parallelism on a 2-layer GEMM MLP-shaped operation

papers.title: Adaptive Space-efficient Collectives for Dynamic and Unstructured Sparsity on GPU Platforms
abstract_claims: sparsity-exploiting all-gather, reduce-scatter, and all-reduce collectives for GPU platforms
bitvector-based Pici format for low-overhead compression and decompression at moderate sparsities
abstract reports up to 5.25x, 2.5x, and 2.66x speedups over NCCL at 99% input sparsity for all-gather, reduce-scatter, and all-reduce respectively

papers.title: DynamiQ: Accelerating Gradient Synchronization using Compressed Multi-hop All-reduce
abstract_claims: compressed multi-hop all-reduce for gradient synchronization
PyTorch DDP extension over NCCL P2P
abstract reports up to 34.2% improvement over compared methods and near-baseline accuracy such as 99.9% of the BF16 baseline

boundaries: Do not treat the abstract-stated latency reductions, speedups, improvement, or accuracy statements as reproduced benchmark results.
Do not promote implementation guidance, production operation claims, product rankings, or NCCL release behavior from these abstracts.
